Literature Digest

Mar 18 – Apr 01, 2026  ·  Chuong Lab, CU Boulder
928 scanned
0 preprints
20/20 journals
keyword + AI

AI Summary

This fortnight's digest is anchored by two Alu element papers with direct relevance to the lab. Loureiro et al. demonstrate that an ATTTC repeat expansion within an intronic Alu element converts a DAB1 intron into a hyperactive neurodevelopmental enhancer causing spinocerebellar ataxia type 37 — establishing Alu elements as gain-of-function disease drivers and complementing the lab's Alu exonization work in IFNAR2. Meanwhile, Genome Biology reveals that inverted Alu pairs form stable intronic RNA duplexes genome-wide, driving divergent exon skipping patterns across primate transcriptomes via HNRNPC — a mechanistic framework directly applicable to how Alu secondary structure controls TE exonization across immune genes.

The cGAS-STING axis dominates the immunity content, with four mechanistic papers: MSH6 governs cGAS subcellular distribution to balance antiviral immunity versus autoimmunity (Yang et al.); COPII vesicle cargo recognition via SEC24C controls STING ER exit (Lyu et al.); monkeypox OPG188 evades immunity by degrading cGAMP (Pan et al.); and TREX1 ER-tethering enables BAF-gated invasion of ruptured micronuclei to suppress immunostimulatory DNA (Chen et al.). These advances are relevant given that TE reactivation generates endogenous nucleic acid ligands for cGAS-STING, and the lab's work on TE enhancers intersects directly with innate sensing of self-DNA.

Curated Highlights 20 papers · 6 topics
